input.instructions: Given a set of polyomino shapes and a grid containing noisy aggregate query results (counts of oil cells in specific regions), develop an algorithm to identify all possible valid placements of the polyominoes that are consistent with the observed aggregate data. Account for the fact that queries are noisy.

variables.selected_prompt_text:
Solve the question directly.
Keep the entire JSON compact.
Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.
Do not narrate your reasoning outside the response field.
In the response field, use exactly two short lines encoded with \n:
FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Use plain text only inside the response field. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or math-style backslashes such as \equiv.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>\nJUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>",
  "result_type": "summary"
}

Question:
Given a set of polyomino shapes and a grid containing noisy aggregate query results (counts of oil cells in specific regions), develop an algorithm to identify all possible valid placements of the polyominoes that are consistent with the observed aggregate data. Account for the fact that queries are noisy.

results.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: Constraint satisfaction algorithm with probabilistic error modeling
JUSTIFICATION: Uses backtracking and probabilistic scoring to handle noisy aggregate data.
